game_name: Bubsy in: Claws Encounters of the Furred Kind

danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat; collision damage to player
Environmental hazard; replaces catnip (censored); causes collision damage or player impairment

danger_signals.visual_form: Increasing enemy density and hazard placement across 16 levels
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating difficulty; world state becomes more hostile as campaign advances

safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation; 500 collected grants extra life; primary player objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Life counter display (Bubsy starts with 9 lives)
safety_signals.meaning: Player safety buffer; death is not permanent until all lives exhausted
Safe passage alternative; added via playtester feedback to provide avoidance routes

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yarn ball collection as territory defense (Earth's yarn supply)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple Woolie variations presumed across 16 levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Invasion premise; intent to steal yarn balls as resource extraction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Yarn balls abundant in environment; peaceful early levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Woolies present but manageable threat; player can collect resources safely

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Woolie density increases; hazard placement escalates across 16 levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bubsy forced to jump on Woolies; defensive action required; banana peels replace natural hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presumed level completion with final boss or Woolie elimination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Yarn ball reclamation; protection of Earth's supply achieved

environmental_storytelling.example: Yarn balls as contested resource between Bubsy and Woolies; establishes stakes and player motivation
Banana peels replace catnip due to Nintendo censorship; reflects regulatory world-state constraints on game content
16-level structure implies invasion deepening; enemy variety and placement density communicate deteriorating safety
Bubsy's catchphrase 'What could possibly go wrong?' signals protagonist confidence despite escalating danger; tonal contrast

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Jumping on Woolies for elimination; offensive posture
Collecting yarn balls; accumulation toward extra lives (defensive preparation)
Presence in levels as obstacles; seeking to steal yarn supply (implied directional threat)
Can be jumped on; presumably defeated by contact from above

notes: Bubsy communicates world-state primarily through resource scarcity (yarn balls under threat), enemy presence escalation, and hazard density. The game lacks explicit faction color coding or environmental faction markers in available documentation; communication relies on sprite design and level structure. Censorship decision (catnip→banana peels) reveals external regulatory world-state affecting game manifestation. The 16-level progression implies narrative escalation from invasion onset to presumed player victory, though specific visual or behavioral shifts per level are undocumented in source material.
